attacking_style: Premeditated breakaway specialist who forces selections early in climbs (before final ascent) to fragment groups. Targets stages identified through reconnaissance, attacking when companions are still recovering from prior efforts.
team_tactics: Operates with independence in breakaways rather than team support. Relies on equipment selection (climbing bikes on targeted stages) and personal positioning to create winning scenarios from long-range moves.
terrain_preference: Multi-climb stages where he can accumulate KOM points and exploit fragmentation opportunities. Thrives on 10+ minute breakaways culminating in 12km+ climbs where attrition works in his favor.
vulnerability: Poor energy management when forced to respond to counters rather than control pace. Gets dropped when others attack after he's already made multiple failed attempts, suggesting limited recovery capacity.
key_patterns: Reconnaissance-driven stage selection, choosing equipment and tactics days in advance (2 instances)
Forces breakaway fragmentation before final climb, not on it (2 instances)
Consistently infiltrates breakaways across multiple stages to accumulate KOM points (Vuelta 2018)
Vulnerable when making multiple attacks without success, leading to dropped status (Tour 2019)
form_trajectory: negative: 2019 Tour showed deteriorated race intelligence with failed multiple attacks and poor energy management, contrasting with calculated 2018 Vuelta success
